J1.basis: Lumina pivoted from a product called InboxZap (email cleanup) to an AI meeting assistant, but the SEO surface never caught up. Your GSC export confirms it — every query with real impression volume (inbox zero, gmail storage...) is 100% legacy InboxZap topic, ranking position 40–65 with near-zero clicks
J1.rationale: Explicitly names the pivot, grounds it in the live site vs. GSC vs. repo split, and frames every recommendation around the new product reality — essentially anchor_10, docked one point because the framing leans on aggregate rather than per-query legacy evidence.

J2.basis: Rewrote all 12 posts from email-cleanup topics to Lumina-relevant ones... same slugs, so any existing indexing/link equity carries over
J2.rationale: Made one blanket disposition (repurpose all 12 in place) justified by aggregate GSC signal, but never walks the inventory asset-by-asset or cites individual impressions/positions to justify differentiated harvest/redirect/sunset calls.

J3.basis: same slugs, so any existing indexing/link equity carries over instead of starting from zero... GSC shows these pages already have real impressions, so they need a reliable discovery path
J3.rationale: Avoids both failure modes — it neither chases inbox-cleanup rankings nor torches equity, instead preserving slugs/sitemap while building the meeting-assistant cluster; but the tension and transition sequencing are handled implicitly rather than argued out.

J1.basis: Lumina pivoted from a product called InboxZap (email cleanup) to an AI meeting assistant, but the SEO surface never caught up... every query with real impression volume... is 100% legacy InboxZap topic... There's currently zero content targeting what Lumina actually sells.
J1.rationale: The pivot is identified explicitly across all three sources (live product, GSC as legacy demand, drifted repo), and every recommendation is framed around the new meeting-assistant reality. Textbook anchor_10.

J2.basis: Rewrote all 12 posts from email-cleanup topics to Lumina-relevant ones... same slugs, so any existing indexing/link equity carries over instead of starting from zero.
J2.rationale: It applies one blanket disposition (repurpose-in-place) to all 12 with a sound equity rationale, but never walks the inventory asset-by-asset or cites per-post impressions/positions to justify why each specific post was repurposed rather than 301'd or sunset — the data reference stays aggregate ('position 40–65').

J3.basis: same slugs, so any existing indexing/link equity carries over instead of starting from zero... GSC shows these pages already have real impressions, so they need a reliable discovery path.
